metadata.summary: Routing index mapping PM tasks to one of 33 methodology slugs in pro/product/product-manager/. First read on cold start; load picked slug next.

content.title: Methodologies Summary (Product Manager)
content.summary: A routing index for all 33 methodologies in pro/product/product-manager/. Not a methodology itself — it answers "which methodology do I need?" for a given PM task and returns a single slug. Read this first when entering product-manager scope without a specific methodology pinned; then load the chosen methodology's own AGENTS.md and execute.

why.p: With 33 methodologies spanning MVP scoping, feature prioritization, roadmaps, discovery, business models, and operations, an agent entering the skill cold has no cheap way to pick the right methodology without a router. This summary provides a one-read decision tree that narrows the search to a single slug before any heavy content is loaded, cutting both token cost and ambiguity.

list.item: Cold start: agent has a PM task and no methodology is pre-selected.
Task triage: deciding between RICE vs MoSCoW, MVP vs MLP, roadmap vs OKR, lifecycle vs feedback management.
Onboarding a new PM or agent to the skill — first read to orient.
Ambiguous task language: stakeholder said "prioritize" or "plan" without naming a framework.
Multiple PM activities bundled in one request — split using the Quick Selection Guide before execution.
Methodology already chosen — skip summary, go straight to that folder's AGENTS.md.
Cross-skill work (BA, PM-agile, project-manager) — use the respective skill's own router.
Execution or templates — look in the chosen methodology's own files, never here.
Strategic/portfolio decisions across multiple products — escalate to product leader, not a single methodology.

how-to.step: Receive PM task. Capture one-sentence intent (verb + object, e.g. "prioritize features", "scope MVP", "validate idea").

Open this file and scan the Quick Selection Guide for a verb match. Pick exactly one row. If two match, prefer the more specific (e.g. "scope minimum product" → minimum-product-frameworks before mvp-scoping).
If no row matches: re-read Core 18 then Extended 15 sections. Still no match → return "out-of-scope" and suggest peer skill (pm-agile, business-analyst, project-manager).
Read picked methodology's methodology.xml or README.md to load templates, checklists, prompts.
If methodology references an agent, dispatch through Sub-agents (see recommended-subagents below).
Execute the methodology's how-to or checklist; collect outputs.
Return to caller: methodology slug used, key outputs, and any deferred items (next slug to run).

list.item: mvp-scoping: Teams build too much before validation. Output: core problem, minimum features, 4-week constraint.
mlp-planning: MVP works but users do not love it. Output: WOW moments, delight features, MLP phases.
feature-prioritization-rice: Too many features, limited resources. Formula: RICE = (Reach x Impact x Confidence) / Effort.
feature-prioritization-moscow: Stakeholders disagree on priorities. Buckets: Must/Should/Could/Won't (60%/20%/10%/0%).
roadmap-design: No clear development direction. Output: Now-Next-Later with milestones and 20% buffer.
user-story-mapping: Features lack user context. Structure: Activities to Tasks to Releases.
release-planning: Unclear work for upcoming sprint. Output: Sprint goal, capacity, velocity.
product-launch: No clear release timeline. Output: Release goal, effort, sprints plus 20% buffer.
okr-setting: Goals are vague or unmeasurable. Framework: Objective plus 3-5 Key Results plus Initiatives.
problem-validation: Building solutions for non-problems. Output: 5+ interviews, competitor analysis, search volume.
product-discovery: Root cause not understood. Method: Five Whys (5 levels deep).
jobs-to-be-done: Features do not match user needs. Format: When [situation], I want [motivation], So I can [outcome].
business-model-research: Business model not structured. Framework: Lean Canvas (9 blocks).
value-proposition-design: Product-market fit unclear. Framework: Customer segment plus Value map.
niche-evaluation: Too many opportunities. Output: Weighted scoring (market size, competition, fit, monetization).
backlog-management: Backlog items unclear or oversized. Principles: DEEP, Definition of Ready.
stakeholder-management: Features disconnected from goals. Chain: Goal to Actor to Impact to Deliverable.
risk-assessment: Unknown risks in product decisions. Method: Impact x Uncertainty matrix, test high-risk first.
continuous-discovery-habits: Building weekly customer touchpoint habits.
feedback-management: Collecting, triaging, and acting on user feedback.
minimum-product-frameworks: Choosing among MVP, MLP, MMP, MAC, RAT, MDP, MVA, MFP, SLC.
mlp-planning: Evolving from MVP to Minimum Lovable Product via 4-layer audit.
competitive-positioning: Market positioning against competitors.
portfolio-strategy: Multi-product portfolio management.
product-led-growth: PLG strategy and implementation.
experimentation-at-scale: Scaling experiments to 100+ per year with statistical rigor.
learning-speed-competitive-moat: Organizational learning velocity as competitive advantage.
product-lifecycle: Introduction, Growth, Maturity, Decline stage management.
product-analytics: Analytics and measurement frameworks.
technical-debt-management: Technical debt registration, scoring, and paydown strategy.
Define MVP: mvp-scoping.
Add delight to MVP: mlp-planning.
Choose right minimum product framework: minimum-product-frameworks.
Choose next feature: feature-prioritization-rice OR feature-prioritization-moscow.
Create roadmap: roadmap-design.
Set team goals: okr-setting.
Validate idea: problem-validation, jobs-to-be-done.
Launch product: product-launch.
Manage backlog: backlog-management.
Build discovery habit: continuous-discovery-habits.
Scale experiments: experimentation-at-scale.
Act on user feedback: feedback-management.
Assess product stage: product-lifecycle.
Address technical debt: technical-debt-management.
Build learning velocity moat: learning-speed-competitive-moat.
Position vs competitors: competitive-positioning.
Manage multi-product portfolio: portfolio-strategy.
Implement PLG motion: product-led-growth.
Set up product metrics: product-analytics.
Identify root cause: product-discovery (Five Whys).
Score market opportunity: niche-evaluation.
Build business model: business-model-research (Lean Canvas).
Refine value proposition: value-proposition-design.
Plan a sprint: release-planning.
Map user journey: user-story-mapping.
Align stakeholders: stakeholder-management.
Assess risk: risk-assessment.

section.name: agentic-role-routing-only
section.p: Use this file as the first read when an agent enters product-manager scope without a specific methodology pinned. Flow:
section.code: PM task arrives → Read methodologies-summary/AGENTS.md → Match task to row in "Quick Selection Guide" → Read knowledge/pro/product/product-manager/<picked>/AGENTS.md → Load that methodology's agent-integration.md (if present) → Execute

list.item: faion-pm-agent: owns mvp-scoping, product-discovery, business-model-research routes.
faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent: invoked from mvp-scoping row.
faion-mlp-agent: invoked from mlp-planning row.
faion-market-researcher-agent: invoked from problem-validation, value-proposition-design, niche-evaluation rows.
faion-persona-builder-agent: invoked from jobs-to-be-done row.

section.name: prompt-pattern-router
section.prompt:
ROLE: You are a PM router agent. You map one PM task to exactly one methodology slug.
INPUTS: - task: <one-sentence PM task from caller> - catalog: 33 slugs listed in methodologies-summary methodology.xml
PROCEDURE: 1. Extract verb + object from task (e.g. "prioritize features", "scope MVP"). 2. Scan Quick Selection Guide rows. Pick the row whose left-hand verb best matches. 3. If two rows match equally, prefer the more specific (e.g. minimum-product-frameworks > mvp-scoping when caller says "choose minimum product type"). 4. If no row matches: return out-of-scope + suggest peer skill (pm-agile | business-analyst | project-manager).
OUTPUT (JSON only, no prose): { "slug": "<single-methodology-slug>", "agent": "<recommended-subagent-or-none>", "next_read": "knowledge/pro/product/product-manager/<slug>/AGENTS.md", "confidence": "high|medium|low", "rationale": "<one-sentence reason>" }

section.name: canonical-template
section.code: # Routing decision record task : <verb + object from caller> matched_row : <row text from Quick Selection Guide> slug : <kebab-case slug> agent : <faion-*-agent or none> next_read : knowledge/pro/product/product-manager/<slug>/AGENTS.md confidence : high | medium | low fallback : <peer skill slug if out-of-scope> notes : <free text, ≤2 lines>

checklist.check: Confirmed task is in product-manager scope (not BA, not pm-agile, not project-manager).
Scanned Quick Selection Guide and matched task to exactly one row.
Wrote a Routing Decision Record (slug, agent, next_read, confidence) before reading anything heavy.
Read picked methodology's AGENTS.md after routing — did not skip ahead to templates.
If two rows tied, picked the more specific slug and recorded rationale.
If no row matched, returned out-of-scope with a peer-skill suggestion instead of forcing a slug.
Did not load multiple methodology bodies in parallel — exactly one slug per route.
Returned the slug to the caller in the final response so the next step can be planned.

verify.step: Run: python3 scripts/validate-methodology-xml.py skills/faion/knowledge/pro/product/product-manager/methodologies-summary/methodology.xml — must print PASS.

Run: ls skills/faion/knowledge/pro/product/product-manager/ — every folder there (excluding SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, workflows, methodologies-summary) must appear by slug somewhere in this file. If a folder is missing, the index has drifted — update it.
Confirm the routing JSON output validates as JSON: echo '<output>' | python3 -c "import sys,json; json.load(sys.stdin)".
Confirm the chosen slug folder exists: test -d skills/faion/knowledge/pro/product/product-manager/<slug> && echo OK.
Confirm picked methodology has an AGENTS.md or methodology.xml file: ls skills/faion/knowledge/pro/product/product-manager/<slug>/.
Caller-side check: the executing agent must report back with the slug it actually used; if it diverges from the routed slug, log a routing miss and update Quick Selection Guide.

list.item: Index is hand-curated — drifts when methodologies are added/removed; the verify step "diff folder vs router" detects drift.
Quick Selection Guide maps tasks to methodologies but not to agents; agent selection must be cross-referenced in each methodology's own agent-integration.md.
No CLI tools, services, or scripts apply at this level — those belong to individual methodologies. The commands block above only loads the router itself.
Confidence calibration is heuristic; on low confidence, prefer reading two candidate methodology AGENTS.md files briefly before committing.
